steps.title: Simplify the expression
steps.content: Simplify $\frac{\sqrt{16x^4y^8}}{x^3}$ by taking the square root of the numerator: $\sqrt{16x^4y^8} = 4x^2y^4$. Then divide by $x^3$: $\frac{4x^2y^4}{x^3} = 4x^{-1}y^4$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $x^2, y^4$: The exponent on $x$ is $+2$, but after dividing $x^2$ by $x^3$ we get $x^{-1}$, not $x^2$. ✗
  • Choice B — $xy^4$: The exponent on $x$ is $+1$, same error as A — ignores the division by $x^3$. ✗
  • Choice C — $x^{-2}y^2$: Both exponents are wrong: $y$ should be $y^4$ (not $y^2$), and $x^{-2}$ would come from taking the square root of $x^4$ twice instead of once. ✗
  • Choice D — $x^{-1}y^4$: Matches $4x^{-1}y^4$ exactly (the constant 4 is not among the variable choices, so we match the variable part). ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
